focus: SF1 predecessor skepticism, Hammer X infomercial debunked, DTC driver criticism

PAIN.quote: I bought the Hammer driver and I can honestly say it took 20-30 yards OFF my drives. It is a pure piece of junk
When I hit a ball it felt like I was hitting a wall of cement. Terrible club
I purchased the hammer x and paid the freight to Australia. I have tried everything to get it to work so have my golfing partners. As far as I am concerned it is a piece of junk
All the good reviews must have been written by family members of the designer. Very disappointed and I wish I lived in the usa so I could return it and get my money back
This club was a scam from the get go. Luckily my buddy only paid $5 at a garage for his

HOPE.quote: I hit one of these my friend owned. My 2007 Burner went 20 yards farther, so the arguments about new tech are bunk along with the shaft argument
HOPE.context: 10 year old mainstream driver outperforms infomercial

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Not only did the Hammer X fail to live up to a single claim made in the infomercial, it actually performed significantly worse than a mainstream competitor
The 2016 Taylormade M1 launched over 10 MPH Faster than the Hammer. So much for the Power Core
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Ball speed way lower than mainstream

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: The increased ball speed from the M1 led to an average carry advantage of over 25 Yards Longer over The Hammer

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: They said in order for this driver to work due to the inverted face you need to tee it over the face by at least an inch... the higher the better! I did that. It actually worked it went 15 yards further than my Callaway epic
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.context: Unusual technique required for infomercial driver

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: Interestingly, I returned the club — after months of telephone calls and badgering, I finally did get a refund

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: The club fixes all slices (yeah, right) but yet they include a training video to tell you how to swing to avoid a slice, if the clubs fixes it, why would I need to change my swing? Snake Oil anyone?
Most anything he is hawking is snake oil. The club looks like it has two sliding weights like TaylorMade started with many years ago

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: TheSandTrap - SF1 Slice Fixing Driver (iacas - site admin/instructor)

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: It fixes your slice by automatically squaring your clubface at impact on the course when it matters. However, when it doesn't matter you'll slice the bejeesus outta it?
This club claims it will make your spouse happy. and take human error out of the equation
Well I guess it's time to trade in my Hammer Turbo Air driver
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Sarcastic comparison to previous infomercial driver

VILLAIN.quote: Three things that debunk the Hammer driver. 1. If the 50 yard increase was real, you would see tour professionals using this club. 50 yards to some means the difference between the PGA tour vs the Web.com tour. There are no tour pros using this club
If the clubs claim were valid, you would see the major manufacturers of clubs copying said design. Only other hack clubs even look close to the hammer
VILLAIN.context: No major OEM copies = proof it doesn't work

VILLAIN.quote: Hamm is in seclusion because he is a conman! Advertisers think golfers are moron's! And they are right!
The Hammer has an unconditional return policy. It also has one of the highest consumer complaint numbers of any info commercial ever made. The BBB rating is an F
If you lived in the USA they charge a $29.95 for returning the club plus a $25 restocking fee. Better off selling it on EBay or something similar. Total junk, and false advertising
VILLAIN.context: Return fees make refunds nearly impossible

VILLAIN.quote: The Hammer Club is GARBAGE!! The commercial is as phony as it gets…DO NOT BUY THIS CLUB

sources_covered: GolfWRX - The hammer driver fake or real or piece of junk
high_value_themes_identified: SF1 predecessor seen as snake oil by golf forums
No tour pros use it = proof it doesn't work
F rating from BBB on Hammer driver
Golf instructor (iacas) guarantees SF1 won't work
Major OEMs not copying design = proof it fails
